<task-id>ab96630ef127b0210</task-id>
<tool-use-id>REDACTED</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/tmp/claude-1001/-home-briney-git-fold-at-scripps/81efea97-4441-45df-9723-649c2ecd0b06/tasks/ab96630ef127b0210.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Re-review Task 4 after fix" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>The diff file shows `envfile.py` as a **new file** (`new file mode 100644`, `--- /dev/null`), meaning this diff package squashes both commits (8e70f17 feat + b048430 fix) into a single "file created" view rather than showing the fix as an incremental patch. That's fine for review purposes — it means I'm looking at the final state directly. Confirming: the final file has no `import re` at all (only `import secrets`, `from collections.abc import Mapping`, `from typing import Any`, `from urllib.parse import urlsplit, urlunsplit`, `import rich`, and the local import). So `re` was correctly removed — no F401 risk, consistent with the "removed `import re`" claim in the fix report.

Now confirming the other originally-approved behaviors are intact in this final diff: never-overwrite (`if paths.env_file.exists(): return False`), real secret (`secrets.token_urlsafe(48)` at write time, no placeholder), and file mode 0o600 (`paths.env_file.chmod(0o600)`) — all present verbatim in the code shown above, unchanged by the fix.

Test quality check on the new regression test:
```python
def test_redact_masks_password_containing_at_symbol():
    out = redact_settings({"database_url": "REDACTED"})
    assert "ss" not in out["database_url"]
    assert "p@ss" not in out["database_url"]
    assert out["database_url"].startswith("REDACTED")
```
This is meaningful: `"ss" not in ...` is the key assertion that would have failed under the old regex (which produced `...fold:***@ss@localhost:5432/db`, where `"ss"` is present). The `startswith` assertion also pins down host/port/path preservation. Good coverage, not just a happy-path re-check.

No remaining doubts justify running the suite myself — the reasoning is fully verified both analytically and empirically against the exact reference case plus adjacent edge cases (no-password, no-credentials, double-`@` password). I'll skip running the full test suite per instructions since the evidence already closes the loop.

---

## Verdict: ✅ Spec-compliant. The Important redaction finding is **RESOLVED**.

### Strengths
- `_redact_db_url` (`src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/envfile.py:66-75`) uses `urlsplit`/`urlunsplit` to parse and reconstruct the URL from typed components (`scheme`, `username`, `hostname`, `port`, `path`, `query`, `fragment`) rather than doing textual substitution — this structurally guarantees the raw password value is never echoed back in any form, including when the password itself contains `@` or multiple `@` characters (verified empirically: `p@ss@word` → fully masked).
- Correctly reasons through `urlsplit` semantics: userinfo/host splits at the **last** `@` in netloc, password splits at the **first** `:` within userinfo — so `p@ss` is captured whole as the password, not truncated at the first `@`.
- Host, port, and path are all preserved unchanged in the reconstructed URL (verified: `localhost:5432/db` unaffected).
- No-password (`postgresql+asyncpg://localhost:5432/db`) and no-credentials-but-username (`postgresql+asyncpg://fold@localhost:5432/db`) cases both return the URL unchanged via the `parts.password is None` early return — no crash, no incorrect mutation.
- `import re` was fully removed (final file only imports `secrets`, `Mapping`, `Any`, `urlsplit`/`urlunsplit`, `rich`, and `FoldappPaths`) — no F401 risk.
- New regression test (`tests/foldapp/test_envfile.py:137-141`) is meaningful: its `"ss" not in ...` assertion is precisely the check that would have failed under the old regex, not a redundant happy-path check.
- Previously-approved behaviors are untouched by the fix: never-overwrite guard (`envfile.py:52`), real generated secret via `secrets.token_urlsafe(48)` at write time (`envfile.py:47,55`), and `chmod(0o600)` (`envfile.py:62`) all remain exactly as originally implemented.

### Issues
None found. No partial-leak paths, no crashes on edge-case URLs, no unused imports, no regressions to the previously-approved contract surface.

**Task quality: Approved.** The fix correctly replaces string-based password truncation with structural URL parsing, closing the `@`-in-password leak for all cases tested (including multi-`@` passwords), while preserving all other spec-mandated behaviors (never-overwrite, real secret, 0o600, exact function names) and leaving no lint/import debris.</result>

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: pending (BASE b558089)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).

## Final whole-branch review
(pending — dispatch on opus after Task 17)

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 5: `units.py` + systemd unit templates, for the fold@Scripps `foldapp` operator CLI.

## Task Description

Read your task brief FIRST — it is your requirements, with the exact code, template files, and commands to use verbatim:
`/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-brief.md`

## Context

`foldapp` is a Typer subpackage at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/` (Tasks 1–4 added shell.py, context.py, state.py, envfile.py, cli.py). This task renders and installs the `systemctl --user` unit files (`fold-api.service`, `fold-scheduler.service`) from templates.

This task CONSUMES `FoldappPaths` (context.py) and `shell.run` (shell.py). It PRODUCES: `render_unit(kind, paths, *, port=8000, uv_path=None, autobio_dir=None) -> str` (kind "api"|"scheduler"; unknown kind → ValueError) and `install_units(paths, *, port=8000, dry_run=False) -> None`.

## CRITICAL ordering + a subtlety — read carefully

1. The brief's Step 3 creates the two template files under `deploy/systemd/` (`fold-api.service.tmpl`, `fold-scheduler.service.tmpl`). You MUST create these template files BEFORE running the render test — otherwise the render will raise FileNotFoundError.
2. `units.py` resolves templates via `_TEMPLATE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "deploy" / "systemd"` — i.e. the REPO ROOT computed from the module's own location, NOT from `paths.app_dir`. This is deliberate: the unit tests pass a stubbed `app_dir` (a tmp dir with no templates), and templates must still be found at the real repo's `deploy/systemd/`. Do not "fix" this to use `paths.app_dir`. Verify `parents[3]` lands on the repo root (units.py is at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/units.py`: parents[0]=foldapp, [1]=fold_at_scripps, [2]=src, [3]=repo root).
3. Templates use `string.Template` `$placeholders` (`$app_dir`, `$env_file`, `$path`, `$uv`, `$port`). Use `.substitute()` (not `.safe_substitute()`).

The rendered PATH must include uv's dir and autobio's dir (computed from `shutil.which` when not passed) plus the standard system dirs, de-duplicated — this is what fixes the known systemd PATH footgun.

## Global constraints (must hold)

- Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings.
- Line length 100. `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run ruff format --check .` must pass.
- `subprocess` never with `shell=True` (all shell-outs go through `shell.run`).
- Tests under `tests/foldapp/`, pytest. Run: `uv run pytest tests/foldapp/test_units.py -v` then `uv run pytest` once before commit.

## Before you begin

If anything is unclear or wrong, ASK. Otherwise proceed.

## Your job

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order (failing test → confirm fail → create templates → implement units.py → confirm pass → commit). Implement exactly what the brief specifies. Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message (it git-adds `deploy/systemd`, `units.py`, and the test).

Work from: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps`

## Report

Write your full report to `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-report.md`:
- what you implemented; files created/changed (including the two .tmpl files)
- TDD evidence: RED (command + failing output + why) and GREEN (command + passing output)
- full-suite + ruff results
- self-review findings; any concerns

Then reply with ONLY (under 15 lines):
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Commits created (short SHA + subject)
- One-line test summary
- Concerns, if any
- The report file path

If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put specifics in your final message.

# Task 5 Review: `units.py` + systemd unit templates

**Spec-compliance verdict: ✅ Compliant**

## Strengths

- `render_unit` and `install_units` signatures match the brief's interface contract exactly, including keyword-only defaults (`port=8000`, `uv_path=None`, `autobio_dir=None`, `dry_run=False`).
- Template directory resolution (`src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/units.py:13`, `_TEMPLATE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "deploy" / "systemd"`) is deliberately independent of `paths.app_dir` — verified by direct execution that `parents[3]` resolves to the actual repo root, so app_dir-stubbing tests still find real templates.
- `_build_path` (`units.py:117-126`) correctly de-duplicates while preserving order (`dict.setdefault` insertion-order trick). Verified empirically: `_build_path('/usr/bin/uv', None)` → `/usr/bin` appears once even though it's also in `_BASE_PATH_DIRS`.
- Edge case "autobio not found" is handled cleanly: verified directly by stripping `PATH` to `/nonexistent` and confirming `shutil.which("autobio")` returns `None`, and the resulting rendered PATH cleanly omits it with no `None`-string leakage or crash.
- `install_units` dry_run threading verified end-to-end: `dry_run=True` writes zero files (`user_unit_dir` never created) and calls `shell.run(..., dry_run=True)` which only prints; `dry_run=False` writes both units correctly with substituted port and PATH.
- `render_unit("bogus", ...)` raises `ValueError` as required — verified directly.
- Templates verified byte-for-byte against the brief: api unit has `ExecStartPre=$uv run alembic upgrade head` + `ExecStart=$uv run foldapp serve --port $port`; scheduler unit has no migration line and `ExecStart=$uv run foldapp scheduler`; both have `WantedBy=default.target`.
- No collision with pre-existing `deploy/fold-api.service` / `deploy/fold-scheduler.service` (system-level units from an earlier commit) — confirmed these live in `deploy/`, the new templates in `deploy/systemd/`, distinct paths.
- `subprocess` is never invoked directly; `install_units` goes through `shell.run` (`units.py:160`), consistent with the global "never `shell=True`" constraint and Task 1's `shell.run` wrapper (confirmed `shell.py` uses `subprocess.run` with list args, `# noqa: S603` justified).
- Single commit (`e812562`), touches exactly the four files named in the brief's Step 6 — no scope creep.
- Reformatted test line confirmed to be a pure line-wrap of the identical `render_unit(...)` call (same positional/keyword args, same assertions below it unchanged) — no weakened assertion.
- Ruff clean on the actual Python files (`ruff check` and `ruff format --check` both pass on `units.py` and `test_units.py`), confirmed by direct re-run.

## Issues

None rise to Critical or Important. One Minor, plan-mandated observation:

- **Minor (plan-mandated).** `units.py:158-159` — in `install_units`, the api and scheduler `render_unit` calls don't share a single resolved `uv_path`/`autobio_dir`; each call independently re-invokes `shutil.which` when not passed. In a pathological environment where PATH changes between the two calls (extremely unlikely in practice, e.g. within microseconds of `install_units` executing) the two units could theoretically get different PATH values. This is exactly the code given verbatim in the brief's Step 4, so it's plan-mandated, not an implementer defect — flagging only for completeness, not actionable within this task's scope.

## Task quality: Approved

Implementation matches the brief verbatim (templates, `units.py`, tests), all consumed interfaces (`FoldappPaths`, `shell.run`) are used correctly and unchanged, edge cases (dedup, autobio-not-found, unknown kind, dry_run) were independently verified by direct execution against the real code rather than taken on faith, and the one deviation from the brief's literal test text (line-wrap for `ruff format`) is confirmed semantically identical. No Critical or Important issues found.</result>

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: pending (BASE b048430)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).

## Final whole-branch review

You are implementing Task 6: `preflight.py` — the environment-check engine behind `foldapp doctor`, for the fold@Scripps `foldapp` operator CLI.

## Task Description

Read your task brief FIRST — it is your requirements, with the exact code and commands to use verbatim:
`/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-6-brief.md`

## Context

`foldapp` is a Typer subpackage at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/` (Tasks 1–5 added shell.py, context.py, state.py, envfile.py, units.py, cli.py). This task adds `preflight.py`: individual environment checks (uv, python, docker, autobio, gpu, ports, env, linger) each returning a `CheckResult`, plus `run_checks` / `has_failures`. Later `foldapp doctor` (Task 10) renders these.

This task CONSUMES `FoldappPaths` (context.py) and `shell.run` (shell.py). It PRODUCES (later tasks depend on exact names): `Status` (StrEnum OK/WARN/FAIL); `CheckResult(name, status, detail, fix)`; the individual `check_*` functions; `run_checks(paths, *, context="deploy") -> list[CheckResult]`; `has_failures(results) -> bool`.

Note: the checks that shell out (`check_docker`, `check_gpu`, `check_linger`) call `shell.run(..., check=False)` so a non-zero exit never raises — they classify the return code. Import ONLY `run` from shell (NOT `CommandError`) — an unused import fails ruff F401.

## Global constraints (must hold)

- Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings.
- Line length 100. `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run ruff format --check .` must pass. (No unused imports.)
- `subprocess` never with `shell=True` (all shell-outs via `shell.run`).
- Tests under `tests/foldapp/`, pytest. Run: `uv run pytest tests/foldapp/test_preflight.py -v` then `uv run pytest` once before commit.

## Before you begin

If anything is unclear or wrong, ASK. Otherwise proceed.

## Your job

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order (failing test → confirm fail → implement → confirm pass → commit). Implement exactly what the brief specifies — nothing more (don't add extra checks beyond those listed). Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message.

Work from: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps`

## Report

Write your full report to `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md` (overwrite any stale content):
- what you implemented; files changed
- TDD evidence: RED (command + failing output + why) and GREEN (command + passing output)
- full-suite + ruff results
- self-review findings; any concerns

Then reply with ONLY (under 15 lines):
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Commits created (short SHA + subject)
- One-line test summary
- Concerns, if any
- The report file path

If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put specifics in your final message.

---

## Spec-compliance verdict: ✅ Compliant

The implementation matches the brief's Step 3 reference code almost verbatim (multi-line reformatting for line length only), and the plan-mandated interfaces are all present with correct names, signatures, and semantics.

## Strengths

- All required exports present with exact names: `Status` (StrEnum OK/WARN/FAIL), `CheckResult(name, status, detail, fix)`, `check_python`, `check_uv`, `check_docker`, `check_autobio`, `check_gpu`, `check_port_free`, `check_env`, `check_linger`, `run_checks`, `has_failures` — all match the brief exactly.
- `check_autobio` correctly WARNs in `context="dev"` and FAILs in `context="deploy"` (`preflight.py:98`); `check_gpu` WARNs (never FAILs) whether Docker is absent (`:99`) or GPU access fails (`:121`) — matches brief.
- `check_uv`/`check_python` correctly FAIL when the tool is missing/version too old — no false leniency.
- Shell-outs (`check_docker`, `check_gpu`, `check_linger`) all call `runner(..., check=False)`, so a non-zero exit is classified rather than raising `CommandError` (`preflight.py:63,106-116,137`).
- Only `run` imported from `fold_at_scripps.foldapp.shell` — no `CommandError` import; confirmed via `grep` — F401-clean.
- `run_checks` includes `check_linger` only when `context == "deploy"` (`preflight.py:177-178`), matching the brief precisely.
- No extra/invented checks beyond the 8 specified — no scope creep (YAGNI respected).
- `check_env`'s bad-secret sentinel strings (`"dev-insecure-secret-change-me"`, `"CHANGE-ME"`) match the actual literals used elsewhere in the codebase (`src/fold_at_scripps/main.py:23`, `deploy/fold.env.example:5`) rather than being arbitrary/inconsistent placeholders.
- `check_port_free` uses `socket.connect_ex` inside a `with` block (proper resource cleanup), non-blocking probe, no `shell=True` anywhere.
- Ruff-verified: `line-length = 100` in `pyproject.toml:42`, `select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP", "B"]`; longest line in the file is 99 chars (measured directly) — no E501 violations remain after the follow-up format commit.
- Test file is a verbatim copy of the brief's Step-1 spec — no deviation, 5/5 tests as reported.

## Issues

**Minor — test coverage gap beyond the plan's minimum.** `tests/foldapp/test_preflight.py` only exercises `check_uv`, `check_autobio`, and `has_failures` (the brief's mandated minimum). `check_python`, `check_docker`, `check_gpu`, `check_port_free`, `check_env`, `check_linger`, and `run_checks` (including the deploy-only `check_linger` inclusion) have zero test coverage. This is plan-mandated (the brief's Step 1 test is exactly what was copied), so it isn't a deviation, but later tasks (e.g., the `doctor` CLI command) will rely on this engine's untested branches (GPU WARN-not-FAIL path, env bad-secret detection, port-in-use WARN). Recommend a follow-up task/commit adds coverage for at least `check_env` (FAIL on missing/bad secret, OK otherwise) and `run_checks` context branching, since those are pure-logic paths that are cheap to test and carry real risk if broken silently.

**Minor — untyped `runner` parameter.** `check_docker`, `check_gpu`, and `check_linger` declare `runner=run` with no type annotation (`preflight.py:59,84,135`), unlike `which: Which = shutil.which` which is typed via the `Which` alias. This is inherited verbatim from the brief's own reference code (not implementer-introduced), so it's a plan-mandated gap rather than a new defect. A `Runner = Callable[..., CommandResult]` alias would have been easy to add and is consistent with the existing `Which` pattern, but this doesn't block approval since it mirrors the spec exactly.

No Critical or Important issues found. `check_gpu`'s hardcoded image tag (`nvidia/cuda:12.4.0-base-ubuntu22.04`) and the network pull it implies is a design choice baked into the brief, not something introduced by the implementer, and is out of scope for this task-level review.

## Task quality: Approved

The diff is a faithful, essentially line-for-line implementation of the brief's Step 3 reference code (differences are purely cosmetic reformatting for the 100-char line limit). All produced interfaces match the exact names and signatures later tasks depend on. Status semantics (autobio dev/deploy split, GPU WARN-not-FAIL, uv/python FAIL-on-missing) are all correctly implemented and directly verifiable in the diff. Shell-out safety (`check=False`, list-args, no `shell=True`) and the F401 import constraint are both satisfied. The two Minor findings (thin test coverage beyond the plan's mandated minimum, untyped `runner` param inherited from the spec) do not rise to "can't be trusted until fixed" — they're reasonable follow-up hardening, not defects blocking this task's integration.</result>

- Test file is a verbatim copy of the brief's Step-1 spec — no deviation, 5/5 tests as reported.

## Issues

**Minor — test coverage gap beyond the plan's minimum.** `tests/foldapp/test_preflight.py` only exercises `check_uv`, `check_autobio`, and `has_failures` (the brief's mandated minimum). `check_python`, `check_docker`, `check_gpu`, `check_port_free`, `check_env`, `check_linger`, and `run_checks` (including the deploy-only `check_linger` inclusion) have zero test coverage. Recommend a follow-up task/commit adds coverage for at least `check_env` (FAIL on missing/bad secret, OK otherwise) and `run_checks` context branching, since those are pure-logic paths that are cheap to test and carry real risk if broken silently.

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: complete (commit e812562, review ✅/sonnet) — units.py render_unit/install_units + deploy/systemd/*.tmpl. Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.
Task 6: pending (BASE e812562)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).

## Final whole-branch review
(pending — dispatch on opus after Task 17)

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 7: `postgres.py` helpers + `set_maintenance_mode`, for the fold@Scripps `foldapp` operator CLI.

## Task Description

Read your task brief FIRST — it is your requirements, with the exact code and commands to use verbatim:
`/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-7-brief.md`

## Context

`foldapp` is a Typer subpackage at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/` (Tasks 1–6 added shell.py, context.py, state.py, envfile.py, units.py, preflight.py, cli.py). This task adds `postgres.py` (compose up, `pg_isready` wait, gzipped `pg_dump`, and a `psql` restore that pipes stdin) and adds `set_maintenance_mode` to the EXISTING `src/fold_at_scripps/system_settings.py`.

This task CONSUMES `FoldappPaths` (context.py) and `shell.run` (shell.py). It PRODUCES (Task 11/13 depend on exact names): `compose_up(paths, *, dry_run=False)`; `wait_ready(paths, *, timeout=30.0, dry_run=False, runner=run, sleep=time.sleep) -> bool`; `dump(paths, dest: Path, *, dry_run=False) -> Path`; `restore(paths, src: Path, *, dry_run=False)`. In `system_settings.py`: `async def set_maintenance_mode(session, enabled: bool) -> None`.

Key details from the brief:
- `restore` does NOT use `shell.run` (which can't stream stdin) — it uses `subprocess.run([...], input=sql, text=True, capture_output=True)` directly (still list args, never `shell=True`), raising RuntimeError on non-zero. Put `import subprocess` at the top of postgres.py.
- `dump` runs pg_dump via `shell.run` (capturing stdout) and writes it through `gzip.open(dest, "wt")`.
- `set_maintenance_mode` reuses the existing `get_system_settings(session)` in that module, sets `.maintenance_mode`, and commits. Append it (don't rewrite the file).
- The integration test (`@pytest.mark.integration`) uses the EXISTING `db_session` fixture from `tests/conftest.py` and auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest already wires this). Async tests work via `asyncio_mode = auto`.

## Global constraints (must hold)

- Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings.
- Line length 100. `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run ruff format --check .` must pass (no unused imports).
- `subprocess` never with `shell=True`.
- Tests under `tests/foldapp/`, pytest. Fast iteration: `uv run pytest tests/foldapp/test_postgres.py -v -m "not integration"`. Before commit run the full `uv run pytest` (the integration test will auto-skip if Postgres isn't running — that's expected and fine; do NOT try to stand up Postgres).

## Before you begin

If anything is unclear or wrong, ASK. Otherwise proceed.

## Your job

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order. Implement exactly what the brief specifies. Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message (it adds postgres.py, system_settings.py, and the test).

Work from: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps`

## Report

Write your full report to `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-7-report.md` (overwrite any stale content):
- what you implemented; files changed
- TDD evidence: RED (command + failing output + why) and GREEN (command + passing output)
- full-suite + ruff results; note whether the integration test ran or auto-skipped
- self-review findings; any concerns

Then reply with ONLY (under 15 lines):
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Commits created (short SHA + subject)
- One-line test summary (note integration skip if applicable)
- Concerns, if any
- The report file path

If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put specifics in your final message.

<result>Local in-function imports are an established pattern in this codebase's test suite (not a new/odd choice). Everything checks out. This confirms compliance across the board. Let me now write the final review.

---

**Spec-compliance verdict: ✅**

All four `postgres.py` functions exist with exact signatures matching the brief; `set_maintenance_mode` was appended correctly; the integration test uses the real `db_session` fixture and `@pytest.mark.integration`; no `shell=True` anywhere; `restore` correctly bypasses `shell.run` for stdin streaming.

## Strengths

- `wait_ready` (`postgres.py:87-109`) correctly implements a bounded poll loop: calls the runner first, checks the deadline second, sleeps last — guarantees at least one attempt, never spins infinitely, and honors `timeout=0.05` in the unit test by returning `False` on the very first failed check without ever invoking `sleep`.
- Gzip round-trip is genuinely compatible: `dump` writes text-mode gzip (`gzip.open(dest, "wt", encoding="utf-8")`, `postgres.py:89`) and `restore` reads text-mode gzip with the same encoding (`postgres.py:hunk`, `gzip.open(src, "rt", encoding="utf-8")`) — no bytes/text mismatch.
- `restore` correctly avoids `shell.run` (which can't stream stdin) and calls `subprocess.run` directly with list args, `input=sql, text=True, capture_output=True`, raising `RuntimeError` with a truncated stderr tail on non-zero exit — matches the brief's explicit constraint verbatim.
- `set_maintenance_mode` is a clean, minimal append (`system_settings.py:36-40`) that reuses `get_system_settings` and commits; existing file content (`get_system_settings`, imports, singleton logic) is untouched.
- `dump`'s non-dry-run path uses `run(...)` (default `check=True`), so a failing `pg_dump` raises before any gzip file is written — no silent creation of a corrupt/empty backup.
- Test infrastructure claims verified directly: `db_session` fixture and `integration` marker/auto-skip exist in `tests/conftest.py:67-94`; `asyncio_mode = "auto"` and the `integration` marker are registered in `pyproject.toml:68-71`; `SystemSettings.maintenance_mode` field exists (`models/system.py:20`).
- No `shell=True` anywhere in the diff; both `subprocess.run` call sites (`shell.py:40`, `postgres.py`) use list args only.
- `ruff check`'s configured `select = ["E","F","I","UP","B"]` (`pyproject.toml:46`) doesn't include bandit's `S` rules, so the `# noqa: S603` comments are inert but harmless — consistent with the pre-existing pattern in `shell.py:40`, not a new inconsistency.

## Issues

None rise to Critical or Important. Two Minor observations:

- **Minor** — `postgres.py`, `dump`/`restore`: real (non-dry-run) subprocess paths for `compose_up`, `dump`, and `restore` have no unit-test coverage (only `wait_ready` both branches and `dump`'s dry-run no-op are tested), leaving the actual `pg_dump`/`psql` invocation and error-raising path (`RuntimeError` on non-zero) exercised only implicitly. This exactly matches the brief's Step 1 test list, though, so it's plan-mandated scope, not a gap introduced by the implementer.
- **Minor** — `# noqa: S603` comments in `postgres.py` are dead weight under the current ruff config (no `S` rules selected) — harmless cosmetic carryover from the brief's literal code, not a functional issue.

## Task quality: Approved

Reasoning: every produced interface matches the exact signature Tasks 11/13 depend on; the timeout loop is correct and terminates; the gzip write/read modes are compatible; `restore` satisfies the brief's explicit "must use subprocess.run directly with stdin, never shell.run" constraint; `set_maintenance_mode` is a faithful, non-destructive append reusing the existing accessor; and all test-fixture/marker/config claims in the report were independently verified against `conftest.py`, `pyproject.toml`, and the `SystemSettings` model rather than taken on faith. No defects found that would make this untrustworthy for downstream tasks.</result>

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: complete (commit e812562, review ✅/sonnet) — units.py render_unit/install_units + deploy/systemd/*.tmpl. Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.
Task 6: complete (commits f85ed47..69f2b8b, review ✅/sonnet) — preflight.py Status/CheckResult + check_* (python/uv/docker/autobio/gpu/port/env/linger) + run_checks(context)/has_failures. autobio WARN(dev)/FAIL(deploy); gpu WARN-not-FAIL; linger only in deploy context; check_env sentinel matches main._DEV_SECRET_KEY. 5 tests, 195 suite, ruff clean.
Task 7: pending (BASE 69f2b8b)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).
- [Minor][T6] preflight tests cover only check_uv/check_autobio/has_failures (brief minimum); check_env/run_checks context-branch/gpu/port untested. Cheap follow-up; doctor CLI (T10) exercises run_checks end-to-end.
- [Minor][T6] check_docker/gpu/linger `runner=run` param untyped (plan-mandated; a Runner alias like Which would be consistent).

## Final whole-branch review

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order. Implement exactly what the brief specifies — nothing more. Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message.

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: complete (commit e812562, review ✅/sonnet) — units.py render_unit/install_units + deploy/systemd/*.tmpl. Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.
Task 6: complete (commits f85ed47..69f2b8b, review ✅/sonnet) — preflight.py Status/CheckResult + check_* (python/uv/docker/autobio/gpu/port/env/linger) + run_checks(context)/has_failures. autobio WARN(dev)/FAIL(deploy); gpu WARN-not-FAIL; linger only in deploy context; check_env sentinel matches main._DEV_SECRET_KEY. 5 tests, 195 suite, ruff clean.
Task 7: complete (commit 3552194, review ✅/sonnet) — postgres.py compose_up/wait_ready(bounded poll)/dump(gzip)/restore(subprocess stdin, not shell.run) + system_settings.set_maintenance_mode (append, reuses get_system_settings). Integration test RAN+passed (Postgres reachable). 199 suite, ruff clean. (Minor: non-dry-run subprocess paths untested per brief; inert noqa — recurring, see T1.)
Task 8: pending (BASE 3552194)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).
- [Minor][T6] preflight tests cover only check_uv/check_autobio/has_failures (brief minimum); check_env/run_checks context-branch/gpu/port untested. Cheap follow-up; doctor CLI (T10) exercises run_checks end-to-end.
- [Minor][T6] check_docker/gpu/linger `runner=run` param untyped (plan-mandated; a Runner alias like Which would be consistent).

## Final whole-branch review
(pending — dispatch on opus after Task 17)

Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.

Task 6: complete (commits f85ed47..69f2b8b, review ✅/sonnet) — preflight.py Status/CheckResult + check_* (python/uv/docker/autobio/gpu/port/env/linger) + run_checks(context)/has_failures. autobio WARN(dev)/FAIL(deploy); gpu WARN-not-FAIL; linger only in deploy context; check_env sentinel matches main._DEV_SECRET_KEY. 5 tests, 195 suite, ruff clean.

Integration test RAN+passed (Postgres reachable). 199 suite, ruff clean. (Minor: non-dry-run subprocess paths untested per brief; inert noqa — recurring, see T1.)

+Task 8: complete (commit 22759e7, review ✅/sonnet) — frontend.py build_frontend (docker build --target dist) + migrate (uv run alembic upgrade head), both cwd=app_dir, injectable runner. 2 tests, 201 suite. (Minor: build_frontend test uses weak assertion per brief; runner untyped — recurring.)

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 9: `service.py` + `run.py` + a `config.api_port` setting, for the fold@Scripps `foldapp` operator CLI.

## Task Description

Read your task brief FIRST — it is your requirements, with the exact code and commands to use verbatim:
`/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-brief.md`

## Context

`foldapp` is a Typer subpackage at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/` (Tasks 1–8 done). This task adds:
- `service.py`: wrappers over `systemctl --user` / `journalctl --user` (`UNIT_NAMES`, `resolve_units`, `systemctl`, `is_active`, `journal`).
- `run.py`: the foreground entry points the systemd units invoke — `serve(host="0.0.0.0", port=None)` (calls `uvicorn.run("fold_at_scripps.main:app", ...)`) and `scheduler()` (delegates to `fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main`).
- `config.py`: add ONE line `api_port: int = 8000` (existing file — insert after `frontend_dist`, don't rewrite the file).

This task CONSUMES `shell.run`. It PRODUCES (Tasks 10/11/12/13 depend on exact names): `service.UNIT_NAMES = {"api": "fold-api", "scheduler": "fold-scheduler"}`; `resolve_units(target) -> list[str]` (target api|scheduler|all; unknown → ValueError); `systemctl(action, target, *, dry_run=False)`; `is_active(unit, *, runner=run) -> bool` (True iff stdout strip == "active"); `journal(target, *, follow)` (uses `os.execvp`); `run.serve(host="0.0.0.0", port=None)`; `run.scheduler()`.

Notes from the brief:
- `journal` intentionally uses `os.execvp` (hands the terminal to journalctl so Ctrl-C behaves), NOT `shell.run`.
- `serve` calls `configure_logging(settings.log_level)` then `uvicorn.run("fold_at_scripps.main:app", host=host, port=port or settings.api_port)`. `uvicorn` is already a runtime dependency.
- `scheduler()` does a local import of `fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main` and calls it.
- The run.py tests mock `uvicorn.run` and `fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main` — so the imports must be resolvable at those mock paths (the brief's test mocks `"uvicorn.run"` and `"fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main"`).

## Global constraints (must hold)

- Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings.
- Line length 100. `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run ruff format --check .` must pass (no unused imports).
- `subprocess` never with `shell=True` (systemctl via `shell.run`; journal via `os.execvp` per brief).
- Tests under `tests/foldapp/`, pytest. Run: `uv run pytest tests/foldapp/test_service.py tests/foldapp/test_run.py -v` then `uv run pytest` once before commit.

## Before you begin

If anything is unclear or wrong, ASK. Otherwise proceed.

## Your job

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order. Implement exactly what the brief specifies. Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message (it adds service.py, run.py, config.py, and the two tests).

Work from: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps`

## Report

Write your full report to `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-report.md` (overwrite any stale content):
- what you implemented; files changed (note the one-line config.py edit)
- TDD evidence: RED (command + failing output + why) and GREEN (command + passing output)
- full-suite + ruff results
- self-review findings; any concerns

Then reply with ONLY (under 15 lines):
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Commits created (short SHA + subject)
- One-line test summary
- Concerns, if any
- The report file path

If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put specifics in your final message.

You are reviewing one task's implementation: spec compliance, then code quality. Task-scoped gate, not a merge review.

## What Was Requested

Read the task brief: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-brief.md`

Global constraints: Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings; line length 100; ruff (E,F,I,UP,B) + format clean (no unused imports); `subprocess` never `shell=True`.
This task PRODUCES (Tasks 10/11/12/13 depend on exact names): `service.UNIT_NAMES = {"api":"fold-api","scheduler":"fold-scheduler"}`; `resolve_units(target)->list[str]` (api|scheduler|all; unknown → ValueError); `systemctl(action, target, *, dry_run=False)` (runs `systemctl --user <action> <units...>` via shell.run); `is_active(unit, *, runner=run)->bool` (True iff stdout.strip()=="active"); `journal(target, *, follow)` (uses `os.execvp`, NOT shell.run — deliberate, to hand the terminal to journalctl); `run.serve(host="0.0.0.0", port=None)` (calls `configure_logging` then `uvicorn.run("fold_at_scripps.main:app", host=host, port=port or settings.api_port)`); `run.scheduler()` (delegates to `fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main`). Also: one added line `api_port: int = 8000` in `config.py` (existing file, inserted, not rewritten).

## What the Implementer Claims They Built

Read the report: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-report.md` (one line wrapped for length; behavior identical).

## Diff Under Review

Base: 22759e7  Head: 355aee0
Diff file: `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/review-22759e7..355aee0.diff`

Read the diff file once — it is your view. Do not re-run git or crawl the codebase. READ-ONLY.

## Do not trust the report

Verify against the diff; a stated rationale never downgrades severity.

## Tests

Implementer reported 7/7 new tests + 208 full suite; 2 warnings pre-existing (confirmed earlier). Don't re-run to confirm. Focused test only for a specific doubt.

## Review

Part 1 — Spec compliance vs brief (Missing/Extra/Misunderstood); ⚠️ if unverifiable. Verify concretely:
- `resolve_units` maps all/api/scheduler and raises ValueError on unknown; `systemctl` passes `--user <action>` + resolved units; `is_active` compares `stdout.strip() == "active"`; `journal` uses `os.execvp` (not shell.run) and builds `journalctl --user -u <unit> [...] [-f]`.
- `serve` uses the app import string `"fold_at_scripps.main:app"` and `port or settings.api_port`; `scheduler()` delegates to the real scheduler main; `config.api_port` added (existing config content intact).
Part 2 — Code quality: the run.py imports must be mockable at `uvicorn.run` and `fold_at_scripps.scheduler.main.main` (check how they're imported — a top-level `import uvicorn` vs `from uvicorn import run` changes the mock path; the brief's test mocks `"uvicorn.run"`); tests verify real behavior. Cite file:line.

## Calibration

Critical/Important/Minor. Important = can't be trusted until fixed. Plan-mandated defects → Important labeled plan-mandated. Strengths first.

## Output format

Begin with spec-compliance verdict (✅/❌/⚠️), then Strengths, then Issues (file:line), then: Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] + reasoning. Your final message IS the report.

- `is_active` compares `stdout.strip() == "active"` against a `runner` default (`service.py:108-110`), correctly using `check=False` so a non-zero `is-active` exit doesn't raise via `shell.run`'s `check=True` default (`shell.py:52-56`).

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: complete (commit e812562, review ✅/sonnet) — units.py render_unit/install_units + deploy/systemd/*.tmpl. Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.
Task 6: complete (commits f85ed47..69f2b8b, review ✅/sonnet) — preflight.py Status/CheckResult + check_* (python/uv/docker/autobio/gpu/port/env/linger) + run_checks(context)/has_failures. autobio WARN(dev)/FAIL(deploy); gpu WARN-not-FAIL; linger only in deploy context; check_env sentinel matches main._DEV_SECRET_KEY. 5 tests, 195 suite, ruff clean.
Task 7: complete (commit 3552194, review ✅/sonnet) — postgres.py compose_up/wait_ready(bounded poll)/dump(gzip)/restore(subprocess stdin, not shell.run) + system_settings.set_maintenance_mode (append, reuses get_system_settings). Integration test RAN+passed (Postgres reachable). 199 suite, ruff clean. (Minor: non-dry-run subprocess paths untested per brief; inert noqa — recurring, see T1.)
Task 8: complete (commit 22759e7, review ✅/sonnet) — frontend.py build_frontend (docker build --target dist) + migrate (uv run alembic upgrade head), both cwd=app_dir, injectable runner. 2 tests, 201 suite. (Minor: build_frontend test uses weak assertion per brief; runner untyped — recurring.)
Task 9: pending (BASE 22759e7)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 1 added an empty `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` in cli.py. Task 12 must MODIFY that existing callback to add the default-status logic — do NOT add a second callback (Typer allows only one).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).
- [Minor][T6] preflight tests cover only check_uv/check_autobio/has_failures (brief minimum); check_env/run_checks context-branch/gpu/port untested. Cheap follow-up; doctor CLI (T10) exercises run_checks end-to-end.
- [Minor][T6] check_docker/gpu/linger `runner=run` param untyped (plan-mandated; a Runner alias like Which would be consistent).

## Final whole-branch review
(pending — dispatch on opus after Task 17)

You are implementing Task 10: wire the `foldapp` CLI commands `doctor`, `version`, `config` (init/show), `serve`, `scheduler`.

## Task Description

Read your task brief FIRST — it is your requirements, with the exact code and commands to use verbatim:
`/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-10-brief.md`

## Context

`foldapp` is a Typer subpackage at `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/` (Tasks 1–9 built shell, context, state, envfile, units, preflight, postgres, frontend, service, run). This task REPLACES `cli.py` with a fuller version wiring the diagnostics/config/run-target commands. Tasks 11–15 will APPEND more commands to this same file later.

IMPORTANT:
- The brief's Step 3 REPLACES the entire `cli.py`. This intentionally removes the empty `@app.callback` that Task 1 added — the brief's new cli.py has NO callback (a default-status callback is added later in Task 12). Do not re-add a callback.
- cli.py must import the submodules as module references — `from fold_at_scripps.foldapp import context, envfile, preflight` — because the tests monkeypatch `preflight.run_checks` and `context.resolve_paths` as module attributes. Keep those as module-qualified calls (`context.resolve_paths()`, `preflight.run_checks(...)`), NOT `from ... import resolve_paths`.
- Heavier imports are LAZY (inside command bodies): `config show` imports `get_settings` inside the function; `serve`/`scheduler` import from `run` inside the function; `version` imports inside. This keeps `--help`/`doctor` free of FastAPI/DB imports.
- `doctor` renders a Rich table and exits non-zero (`raise typer.Exit(code=1)`) when `preflight.has_failures` is true.

This task CONSUMES context/envfile/preflight/service/run/shell from earlier tasks. It PRODUCES the CLI commands the reviewer/tests check.

## Global constraints (must hold)

- Python 3.11+; `from __future__ import annotations`; type hints; Google-style docstrings.
- Line length 100. `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run ruff format --check .` must pass.
- CLI = Typer; output = Rich.
- Tests under `tests/foldapp/`, pytest. The brief APPENDS tests to `tests/foldapp/test_cli.py`. Run: `uv run pytest tests/foldapp/test_cli.py -v` then `uv run pytest` once before commit. Also sanity-check `uv run foldapp doctor --dev` runs (it may print WARN/FAIL rows — that's fine; just shouldn't crash).

## Before you begin

If anything is unclear or wrong, ASK. Otherwise proceed.

## Your job

Follow the brief's TDD steps in order. Implement exactly what the brief specifies. Make ruff clean before committing. Use the brief's commit message.

Write your full report to `/home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/.superpowers/sdd/task-10-report.md` (overwrite any stale content):
- what you implemented; files changed
- TDD evidence: RED (command + failing output + why) and GREEN (command + passing output)
- full-suite + ruff results; note whether `foldapp doctor --dev` ran cleanly
- self-review findings; any concerns

Then reply with ONLY (under 15 lines):
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Commits created (short SHA + subject)
- One-line test summary
- Concerns, if any
- The report file path

If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put specifics in your final message.

## Spec-compliance verdict: ✅

The implementation matches the brief precisely, with only trivial, justified reformatting (line-length fixes) and one reasonable, disclosed test-assertion change.

## Strengths

- `cli.py` fully replaced; `@app.callback` is gone with no leftover references anywhere in the tree (confirmed via `grep -rn "_cli_callback\|app.callback" src/ tests/` — the only hit is the unrelated top-level `src/fold_at_scripps/cli.py`).
- All six commands present and correctly wired: `doctor`, `version`, `config init`, `config show` (via `config_app.command(...)`), `serve`, `scheduler`. `config_app` is created and mounted with `app.add_typer(config_app, name="config")` (`src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/cli.py:13-14` in diff, i.e. lines 28-30 of the new file).
- Module-reference imports used correctly: `from fold_at_scripps.foldapp import context, envfile, preflight`, and all call sites are module-qualified (`context.resolve_paths()`, `preflight.run_checks(...)`, `preflight.has_failures(...)`, `envfile.scaffold_env(...)`, `envfile.redact_settings(...)`). Verified this is required and sufficient for the tests' `monkeypatch.setattr(preflight, "run_checks", ...)` and `monkeypatch.setattr(context, "resolve_paths", ...)` patterns to take effect — confirmed by actually running `test_help_lists_command_group`, which passed.
- Lazy imports verified empirically, not just by inspection: `uv run python -c "import fold_at_scripps.foldapp.cli; ..."` shows none of `fastapi`, `sqlalchemy`, `asyncpg`, `uvicorn` land in `sys.modules` after import. `get_settings` (in `config_show`), `run.serve`/`run.scheduler` (in `serve`/`scheduler`), and `importlib.metadata.version`/`shell.run` (in `version`) are all imported inside function bodies as required.
- `doctor` builds a `rich.table.Table("Check", "Status", "Detail", "Fix")`, populates rows with a status-colored cell, and raises `typer.Exit(code=1)` iff `preflight.has_failures(results)` — matches brief exactly.
- Cross-checked every downstream interface actually used by the new `cli.py` against its real implementation (not just the brief's assumed signatures): `preflight.run_checks(paths, *, context="deploy")`, `preflight.has_failures(results)`, `context.resolve_paths(...)` (all-keyword, all-optional), `shell.run(...)` returns an object with `.stdout`, `envfile.scaffold_env(paths) -> bool`, `envfile.redact_settings(mapping) -> dict`, `run.serve(host=..., port=...)`, `run.scheduler()`. All match the call sites in the new `cli.py` with correct argument names/types. `Status` is a `StrEnum`, so `_STATUS_STYLE[r.status]` dict lookup works correctly (verified interactively).
- The `test_help_lists_command_group` change (`"foldapp" in output` → `"doctor" in output`) is reasonable: it's a direct, unavoidable consequence of the brief's own verbatim help-string change (which drops the `"foldapp: "` prefix), it was disclosed prominently in the report, and the new assertion still verifies the original intent — that `--help` lists a real registered command. Ran the test standalone; it passes for the right reason (Typer's help output legitimately lists `doctor` in the `Commands:` section).
- Diff is minimal and scoped: `git diff --stat` confirms only the two expected files changed, matching the review diff exactly.
- Ruff check + format independently re-run on just the two changed files: clean.

## Issues

None found at Critical or Important severity.

Minor:
- `src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/cli.py` (`version` command): if `paths.app_dir` is not a git repository (e.g., a fresh install before `git clone`, or a tarball deploy), `shell.run(["git", "rev-parse", ...], check=False)` will return a non-zero exit with empty `stdout`, and the code correctly falls back to `"unknown"` — this is handled properly, not a bug, noting only because it's the one path with external-process dependency inside a command that's otherwise well-covered by `test_version_smoke`. No fix needed.
- The report's two disclosed "whitespace-only" reformats (splitting `version()`'s `run(...)` call and switching `config_init()`'s message to `if/else`) are purely cosmetic and behavior-preserving — confirmed by reading the diff; not a concern.

## Task quality: Approved

Spec compliance is exact where it matters (command set, sub-app wiring, module-reference imports for monkeypatching, lazy imports, doctor's table + exit code), and the one deviation from the brief's literal text (the pre-existing help-text assertion) is disclosed, narrowly scoped, and verified to still test something real. All interface assumptions in the new `cli.py` were cross-checked against the actual implementations of `preflight`, `context`, `envfile`, `shell`, and `run` rather than trusting the brief's snippet blindly, and all matched. Ruff and the targeted test were independently re-verified rather than taken on faith from the report.</result>

## Spec-compliance verdict: ✅

The implementation matches the brief precisely, with only trivial, justified reformatting (line-length fixes) and one reasonable, disclosed test-assertion change.

## Strengths

- `cli.py` fully replaced; `@app.callback` is gone with no leftover references anywhere in the tree (confirmed via `grep -rn "_cli_callback\|app.callback" src/ tests/` — the only hit is the unrelated top-level `src/fold_at_scripps/cli.py`).
- All six commands present and correctly wired: `doctor`, `version`, `config init`, `config show` (via `config_app.command(...)`), `serve`, `scheduler`. `config_app` is created and mounted with `app.add_typer(config_app, name="config")` (`src/fold_at_scripps/foldapp/cli.py:13-14` in diff, i.e. lines 28-30 of the new file).
- Module-reference imports used correctly: `from fold_at_scripps.foldapp import context, envfile, preflight`, and all call sites are module-qualified (`context.resolve_paths()`, `preflight.run_checks(...)`, `preflight.has_failures(...)`, `envfile.scaffold_env(...)`, `envfile.redact_settings(...)`). Verified this is required and sufficient for the tests' `monkeypatch.setattr(preflight, "run_checks", ...)` and `monkeypatch.setattr(context, "resolve_paths", ...)` patterns to take effect — confirmed by actually running `test_help_lists_command_group`, which passed.
- Lazy imports verified empirically, not just by inspection: `uv run python -c "import fold_at_scripps.foldapp.cli; ..."` shows none of `fastapi`, `sqlalchemy`, `asyncpg`, `uvicorn` land in `sys.modules` after import. `get_settings` (in `config_show`), `run.serve`/`run.scheduler` (in `serve`/`scheduler`), and `importlib.metadata.version`/`shell.run` (in `version`) are all imported inside function bodies as required.
- `doctor` builds a `rich.table.Table("Check", "Status", "Detail", "Fix")`, populates rows with a status-colored cell, and raises `typer.Exit(code=1)` iff `preflight.has_failures(results)` — matches brief exactly.
- Cross-checked every downstream interface actually used by the new `cli.py` against its real implementation (not just the brief's assumed signatures): `preflight.run_checks(paths, *, context="deploy")`, `preflight.has_failures(results)`, `context.resolve_paths(...)` (all-keyword, all-optional), `shell.run(...)` returns an object with `.stdout`, `envfile.scaffold_env(paths) -> bool`, `envfile.redact_settings(mapping) -> dict`, `run.serve(host=..., port=...)`, `run.scheduler()`. All match the call sites in the new `cli.py` with correct argument names/types. `Status` is a `StrEnum`, so `_STATUS_STYLE[r.status]` dict lookup works correctly (verified interactively).
- The `test_help_lists_command_group` change (`"foldapp" in output` → `"doctor" in output`) is reasonable: it's a direct, unavoidable consequence of the brief's own verbatim help-string change (which drops the `"foldapp: "` prefix), it was disclosed prominently in the report, and the new assertion still verifies the original intent — that `--help` lists a real registered command. Ran the test standalone; it passes for the right reason (Typer's help output legitimately lists `doctor` in the `Commands:` section).
- Diff is minimal and scoped: `git diff --stat` confirms only the two expected files changed, matching the review diff exactly.
- Ruff check + format independently re-run on just the two changed files: clean.

None found at Critical or Important severity.

Minor:

# foldapp Operator CLI — progress ledger

Branch: feature/foldapp-cli  (base: main @ bb0e0cd; plan/spec commits 14b944c, 9c505b5)
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli.md  (17 tasks)
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-foldapp-cli-design.md
Execution: subagent-driven-development (fresh implementer + task review per task).

Env notes: unit tests run without Postgres; the one integration test (Task 7
maintenance toggle) auto-skips when Postgres is down (conftest). Fast iteration:
`uv run pytest -m "not integration"`. Gate: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .`.

## Tasks
Task 1: complete (commit 3a90788, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — foldapp package scaffold + shell.run/CommandResult/CommandError + Typer cli.app/main + console script (foldapp only; old fold-admin/fold-scheduler entries removed) + rich dep. 5/5 foldapp, 177/177 suite, ruff clean.
Task 2: complete (commit e49a073, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — context.py FoldappPaths + resolve_paths (env/home/app_dir/user overrides; FOLDAPP_APP_DIR/FOLDAPP_STATE_DIR). 3 tests, 180 suite, ruff clean.
Task 3: complete (commit b558089, review ✅ clean/sonnet) — state.py DeployState + read_state/write_state (JSON, parent-dir create, missing→None). 2 tests, 182 suite. Reviewer isolated the suite's "2 warnings" to pre-existing code, NOT this diff.
Task 4: complete (commits 8e70f17..b048430, review ✅/sonnet) — envfile.py generate_secret_key/scaffold_env(never-overwrite,0o600)/redact_settings. FIX applied (Important, was plan-mandated): DB-URL password redaction now uses urllib.parse (was regex leaking passwords containing '@') + regression test. 5 envfile tests, 187 suite, ruff clean.
Task 5: complete (commit e812562, review ✅/sonnet) — units.py render_unit/install_units + deploy/systemd/*.tmpl. Template dir from __file__ parents[3] (not app_dir); PATH deduped incl uv+autobio dirs (fixes Plan 10 footgun); api has migration ExecStartPre, scheduler doesn't; WantedBy=default.target. 3 tests, 190 suite, ruff clean. NOTE: old deploy/fold-*.service (system units) still present — removed in Task 16.
Task 6: complete (commits f85ed47..69f2b8b, review ✅/sonnet) — preflight.py Status/CheckResult + check_* (python/uv/docker/autobio/gpu/port/env/linger) + run_checks(context)/has_failures. autobio WARN(dev)/FAIL(deploy); gpu WARN-not-FAIL; linger only in deploy context; check_env sentinel matches main._DEV_SECRET_KEY. 5 tests, 195 suite, ruff clean.
Task 7: complete (commit 3552194, review ✅/sonnet) — postgres.py compose_up/wait_ready(bounded poll)/dump(gzip)/restore(subprocess stdin, not shell.run) + system_settings.set_maintenance_mode (append, reuses get_system_settings). Integration test RAN+passed (Postgres reachable). 199 suite, ruff clean. (Minor: non-dry-run subprocess paths untested per brief; inert noqa — recurring, see T1.)
Task 8: complete (commit 22759e7, review ✅/sonnet) — frontend.py build_frontend (docker build --target dist) + migrate (uv run alembic upgrade head), both cwd=app_dir, injectable runner. 2 tests, 201 suite. (Minor: build_frontend test uses weak assertion per brief; runner untyped — recurring.)
Task 9: complete (commit 355aee0, review ✅/sonnet, zero issues) — service.py (UNIT_NAMES/resolve_units/systemctl/is_active/journal-via-execvp) + run.py (serve→uvicorn.run main:app; scheduler→scheduler.main.main lazy import) + config.api_port=8000. 7 tests, 208 suite.
Task 10: pending (BASE 355aee0)

## Cross-task notes (controller must apply)
- Task 10 fully REPLACES cli.py (removing the empty `@app.callback` Task 1 added). After Task 10 there is NO callback. Task 12 adds exactly ONE `@app.callback(invoke_without_command=True)` (default → status). Tasks 11/12/13/14/15 APPEND commands to the Task-10 cli.py (don't rewrite it).

## Minor findings (for final whole-branch review triage)
- [Minor][T1] shell.py: `# noqa: S603` is inert (S rules not selected). Drop or replace with a plain comment. (Same pattern is copied into later tasks' subprocess calls.)
- [Minor][T1] cli.py help text set to "foldapp: fold@Scripps operator CLI …" to satisfy the smoke test (brief said just "fold@Scripps operator CLI …").
- [Minor][T1] shell.py cwd/env/capture=False paths untested (mechanically reviewed; exercised indirectly by later tasks).
- [Minor][T2] context._find_app_dir upward-walk + CWD fallback untested and unbounded (plan-mandated; at runtime systemd WorkingDirectory=app_dir or FOLDAPP_APP_DIR covers it).
- [Minor][T3] state.read_state doesn't guard malformed/corrupt JSON (raises instead of None). Relevant to Task 13 rollback path — corrupt state file would crash rather than fail gracefully.
- [Minor][T4] redact_settings typed Mapping[str, Any]/dict[str,Any] vs brief's Mapping[str, object]/dict[str, object]. Functionally fine; note for consistency.
- [Minor][T4] envfile.scaffold_env dry_run branch untested (exercised later via CLI config init).
- [Minor][T6] preflight tests cover only check_uv/check_autobio/has_failures (brief minimum); check_env/run_checks context-branch/gpu/port untested. Cheap follow-up; doctor CLI (T10) exercises run_checks end-to-end.
- [Minor][T6] check_docker/gpu/linger `runner=run` param untyped (plan-mandated; a Runner alias like Which would be consistent).

## Final whole-branch review
(pending — dispatch on opus after Task 17)
